core_mechanism: a systematic asymptotic expansion in the inverse of a large size/dimension parameter (1/N), in which the leading order collapses to an exactly solvable saddle-point/mean-field limit and successive corrections form an ordered power series, combined with a double-scaling limit that ties the expansion parameter to a critical point
brief_reason: The abstract centers on the 1/N expansion of matrix models, presenting large-N solutions and their double-scaling properties as the organizing machinery for otherwise intractable systems.
